target.title: fix(agents): keep lightweight cron prompts out of no-op compaction
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96658.

Lightweight isolated cron runs on very small context windows can reserve too much of the model window before the first prompt is sent. In the reported 4,096-token fallback-model case, current `main` leaves only 2,048 prompt tokens for a 3,544-token lightweight cron prompt, so the precheck routes to `compact_only` even though the lightweight bootstrap has no useful history to compact.

## Why This Change Was Made

This keeps the shared small-context prompt floor conservative: `MIN_PROMPT_BUDGET_RATIO` remains `0.5` for full-context callers, settings reserve capping, CLI compaction, mid-turn guard paths, image prompts, and lightweight shared-history runs.

The higher 87.5% prompt share is scoped to history-free lightweight pre-prompt admission. Before accepting `fits`, the precheck subtracts active tool-schema pressure. For OpenAI Responses and Completions routes, that pressure is estimated from the same provider-visible tool payload shape, strict flag, and strict-normalized parameters used by the request transport.

No SDK shape, config, docs, workflow, or global budget policy is changed.

## User Impact

Lightweight cron jobs on small-context fallback models can proceed when the rendered prompt and active tool schema surface fit the model window, instead of entering no-op compact-only recovery with nothing useful to compact.

Tool-heavy runs, prompt-image runs, shared-history lightweight runs, and non-lightweight/full-context paths keep conservative behavior.

## Evidence

Current-head behavior proof for the reported 4,096-token / 20,000-reserve boundary:

```text
history-free lightweight, no tools: route=fits, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=3584, effectiveReserveTokens=512
history-free lightweight, OpenAI Responses/Completions provider-shaped tool payloads: provider-visible wrappers and strict-normalized schemas are budgeted before fits
history-free lightweight, prompt image/shared history/full mode: keep the shared 2048-token floor on 4k models
```

Focused validation on head `7f204b6e2b4`:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.tools-allow.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts
# PASS: 4 files, 97 tests

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# PASS

node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts
# PASS

git diff --check
# PASS
```

Current-head real cron proof on this PR branch used a temp `HOME` / `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, local mock OpenAI Responses provider, `openai/gpt-5.5` configured with `contextWindow=4096` / `contextTokens=4096`, `agents.defaults.compaction.reserveTokens=20000`, isolated `--light-context`, and restricted `--tools read`. The prompt is above the old 2,048-token floor and below the current provider-visible tool budget:

```text
git rev-parse --short HEAD
# 7f204b6e2b4

node /tmp/openclaw-current-head-cron-proof.mjs /Users/fallmonkey/Developer/openclaw-worktrees/96658-lightweight-cron-budget
# status=ok
# route=fits
# estimatedPromptTokens=3144
# promptBudgetBeforeReserve=3316
# overflowTokens=0
# reserveTokens=20000
# effectiveReserveTokens=512
# contextTokenBudget=4096
# messages=0
# unwindowedMessages=0
# responsesRequests=2
```

The unrestricted/default tool surface is intentionally not claimed by this proof; with provider-visible budgeting, tool-heavy 4k runs remain conservative instead of false-fitting near the model boundary.

Review comments addressed:

- Codex / ClawSweeper image follow-up: prompt-image lightweight runs stay on the shared floor.
- ClawSweeper tool follow-up: OpenAI Responses/Completions tool pressure now uses provider-visible tool payloads before accepting `fits`.
- ClawSweeper proof follow-up: real isolated `lightContext` cron proof above was rerun against the current source worktree after the tool-budgeting change.
- Scope reduction: the implementation remains inside the existing pre-prompt helper plus its caller; no SDK/config/docs/workflow/global policy changes.

Autoreview: attempted one local draft-PR review round earlier, but the helper failed before review due local Codex config parsing (`service_tier = priority`; helper expected `fast` or `flex`). No autoreview findings were produced.

Agent transcript: a matching local session was found, but no transcript is included because adding sanitized transcript logs requires explicit approval.
target.author: monkeyleet
